headlines.text: Stephen Colbert signs off tonight in final Late Show broadcast after CBS cancellation
Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump warns deal must come in 'a few days or it goes very quickly'
SpaceX files S-1 for what could be the largest IPO in history, targeting up to $2 trillion valuation
Memorial Day gas prices hit four-year highs at $4.56/gal as Strait of Hormuz stays closed
Stocks slide as oil tops $101 and Treasury yields climb; Nvidia earnings fail to lift markets
Meta lays off 8,000 workers — 10% of staff — and reassigns thousands more to AI roles
Iran announces 'Controlled Maritime Zone' in Strait of Hormuz as diplomatic clock ticks
Colorado Gov. Polis censured by state Democratic Party for freeing election denier Tina Peters
SpaceX Starship Flight 12 set for 6:30 PM ET launch from Texas — biggest rocket ever built
The Mandalorian and Grogu hits theaters tomorrow as first Star Wars film since 2019

breaking.text: TONIGHT: Stephen Colbert airs his final Late Show at 11:35 PM ET — both Jimmy Fallon and Jimmy Kimmel are going dark in solidarity.

stories.hed: Iran Reviews US Peace Proposal, IRGC Claims Hormuz Control, Trump Says Clock Is Ticking
stories.dek: Pakistan is still playing diplomatic matchmaker between Washington and Tehran, passing notes like it's seventh grade. Iran says it will 'downblend' its own enriched uranium, which is the diplomatic equivalent of 'we'll clean our own room, thanks.' Trump gave them two to three days, which in Iran-war time is basically a geological epoch.

stories.hed: USS Nimitz Arrives in Southern Caribbean as Cuba Promises 'Fierce Resistance'
stories.dek: The US sent its aircraft carrier to hover near Cuba as a 'show of force,' while Secretary Rubio offered $100 million in food and medicine — a classic stick-and-carrot combo, if the carrot is delivered by an aircraft carrier. Cuba responded by promising fierce resistance, which tracks.

stories.hed: SpaceX Files Blockbuster IPO at Up to $2 Trillion Valuation — And Musk's Pay Is Tied to a Mars Colony
stories.dek: SpaceX dropped its S-1 and it is a document full of moon-shot promises, including the 'largest actionable total addressable market in human history' at $28.5 trillion. Musk's compensation package includes bonus shares if he colonizes Mars with at least one million people, which is either visionary or the most audacious severance package in corporate history.

stories.hed: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs and Moves Thousands to AI — Zuckerberg Says No More Layoffs This Year
stories.dek: Meta laid off roughly 10% of its workforce while simultaneously promising no more company-wide layoffs — a sentence that only makes sense if you are Mark Zuckerberg. The workers reassigned to AI initiatives were reportedly told their new jobs are to help build the AI that will eventually replace the remaining workers.

stories.hed: CBS Canceled Colbert, So Tonight the Only Remaining Counterweight to Prime-Time Propaganda Goes Dark
stories.dek: CBS cited 'financial reasons' for canceling The Late Show, though Colbert himself noted the network's change of heart coincided with Trump's lawsuit pressure on Paramount. The replacement: Byron Allen's Comics Unleashed, which promises to be non-political and non-offensive — perfect for a country that is currently at war and paying $4.56 for gas.

markets.context: Oil topping $101 reignited inflation fears and pushed Treasury yields higher, drowning out Nvidia's blockbuster earnings and dragging all major indices into the red.

gas.note: Gas is now $1.38 higher than a year ago — congrats on your Memorial Day road trip, enjoy the $180 fill-up.

popCulture.hed: Colbert's Final Late Show Airs Tonight — Fallon and Kimmel Go Dark in Solidarity
popCulture.dek: After 11 years and a controversial cancellation by Paramount-owned CBS, Stephen Colbert takes his final bow at 11:35 PM ET tonight in what CBS is billing as an 'extended' episode. Bruce Springsteen already showed up Wednesday to torch the place on the way out, performing an anti-ICE anthem and directly blaming Trump for the cancellation — so the bar for tonight is set somewhere between a state funeral and a punk show.

genZ.term: Devil Wears Prada Miranda Priestly 'and Emily... that's all' trend
genZ.definition: Spawned by the massive box-office return of The Devil Wears Prada 2, TikTokers are using Miranda Priestly's icily dismissive 'and Emily... that's all' audio to film two contrasting shots — one polished and correct, one absolute chaos — as a subtle judgment format. It exploded this week after the sequel pulled in $43 million in its second weekend and Priestly-core energy saturated the FYP.
genZ.usage: Iran reviewing the US peace proposal for the fifth day in a row: [shot of Iran's foreign ministry] 'and Emily...' [cut to Trump at Andrews giving a two-day deadline] 'that's all.'

tldr: Iran is still reviewing a US peace proposal while Trump's clock ticks, oil is above $101, gas hit a four-year high at $4.56, and Stephen Colbert signs off tonight after CBS canceled the one late-night show that wasn't afraid to say what everyone was thinking. SpaceX filed for the biggest IPO in history and plans to colonize Mars, which at current gas prices might actually be the more affordable commute.
